content: Just as the human brain relies on prefrontal-hippocampal interaction to dynamically retrieve contextually relevant memories for active learning, autonomous AI agents can be redesigned to use Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) as a built-in biological-inspired memory system that proactively identifies and retrieves missing knowledge to drive curiosity-driven active learning, rather than just passively augmenting static generation outputs. This creates a closed-loop learning agent that grows its knowledge base autonomously by coupling RAG's retrieval to the agent's intrinsic curiosity signal.
